query: OPNsense vs pfSense for a small branch site firewall

metrics.content:
I’ll route this to the right network-desk specialist first, then give a concise comparison.

**Short answer:** for a small branch firewall, choose **pfSense** if you want the safer, more established appliance ecosystem; choose **OPNsense** if you value faster updates, a cleaner UI, and stronger built-in automation/API.

| Area | OPNsense | pfSense |
|---|---|---|
| Best fit | Modern branch firewall with automation needs | Conservative branch firewall with broad community history |
| UI/API | Cleaner UI, stronger REST API | Mature UI, less API-forward |
| Updates | More frequent | More conservative, especially pfSense Plus |
| Packages | Good plugin ecosystem | Strong packages like pfBlockerNG, Snort/Suricata |
| HA | CARP/pfsync supported | CARP/pfsync supported; widely deployed |
| Commercial path | Deciso support/appliances | Netgate pfSense Plus/appliances |
| Operational risk | Faster change cadence may need more testing | More predictable for conservative ops |

**Recommendation:** for a typical small branch doing WAN edge, NAT, site-to-site VPN, VLAN segmentation, DHCP/DNS relay, and basic IDS/blocklists, I’d default to **pfSense Plus on Netgate hardware** if supportability and stability matter most. I’d pick **OPNsense** if you want open-source-first operations, API-driven management, or prefer its UI/update model.

Reference the official docs before deciding: [OPNsense documentation](https://docs.opnsense.org/) and [pfSense documentation](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/).

> Analysis only — verify against vendor documentation before applying.
